architecture_fit.note: Minimal single hand-set attention head (no MLP/value), exactly base_model.py reduced; hits ~0.80-0.86 attn_at_k across the sweep, but reframes 'positional' selection as a content pointer keyed on the marker, which is a defensible-but-real sidestep of pure position addressing.

baseline_comparison.note: Three well-chosen contrasts: uniform 1/L, fixed-position@8 strawman (nails k=8, fails elsewhere), and the unfaithful cross-seq oracle as an explicit upper bound.

faithfulness.note: Causal ablations zero the query and the marker key-channel, both collapsing the head to 1/L, isolating the marker-keyed query as the driver of selection.

operating_range.note: Sweeps spurious-marker rate r from 0.0 to 0.50 with empirical accuracy sitting on the closed-form Bayes ceiling, plus full k-sweep, characterizing degradation analytically.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Fully hand-built circuit: q = beta*onehot(99), one-hot key embeddings, softmax — reproduces the target behaviour by hand at the analytic ceiling.

visual_judgement.note: Four legible views; the default sweep-accuracy plot overlays methods, Bayes ceiling and uniform baseline so the whole claim reads at a glance.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties axis (attn_at_k = scored quantity), the ceiling, and baselines directly to the claim; explains why each view is the minimal artefact that would break if wrong.

notes: A rigorous, intellectually honest attempt that corrects first_pass's cross-sequence cheat by computing a faithful per-sequence content pointer and proving the Bayes-optimal ceiling (~0.86) is the real limit. Excellent baselines, causal ablations, operating-range characterization, and a fully hand-built circuit. Only caveat: it resolves the goal's 'positional' selection via content addressing on the marker — well-argued as the only faithful per-sequence handle, but a slight reframing of the literal task.
